title: amsinckia helleri
intro: Amsinckia helleri, also known as Heller's amsinckia or western yarrowleaf, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Amsinckia helleri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote skin healing, as well as its potential to act as a natural repellent against insects and parasites. Herbal preparations of Amsinckia helleri can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots to make a tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
